# assert

```go
import "github.com/tj/assert"
```

Package assert implements the same assertions as the \`assert\` package but stops test execution when a test fails.

### Example Usage

The following is a complete example using require in a standard test function:

```
import (
  "testing"
  "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestSomething(t *testing.T) {

  var a string = "Hello"
  var b string = "Hello"

  require.Equal(t, a, b, "The two words should be the same.")

}
```

### Assertions

The \`require\` package have same global functions as in the \`assert\` package, but instead of returning a boolean result they call \`t.FailNow\(\)\`.

Every assertion function also takes an optional string message as the final argument, allowing custom error messages to be appended to the message the assertion method outputs.

<a name="Condition"></a>

## func [Condition](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L17>)

```go
func Condition(t TestingT, comp assert.Comparison, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Condition uses a Comparison to assert a complex condition.

<a name="Contains"></a>

## func [Contains](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L29>)

```go
func Contains(t TestingT, s interface{}, contains interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Contains asserts that the specified string, list\(array, slice...\) or map contains the specified substring or element.

```
assert.Contains(t, "Hello World", "World")
assert.Contains(t, ["Hello", "World"], "World")
assert.Contains(t, {"Hello": "World"}, "Hello")
```

<a name="DirExists"></a>

## func [DirExists](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L37>)

```go
func DirExists(t TestingT, path string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

DirExists checks whether a directory exists in the given path. It also fails if the path is a file rather a directory or there is an error checking whether it exists.

<a name="ElementsMatch"></a>

## func [ElementsMatch](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L48>)

```go
func ElementsMatch(t TestingT, listA interface{}, listB interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

ElementsMatch asserts that the specified listA\(array, slice...\) is equal to specified listB\(array, slice...\) ignoring the order of the elements. If there are duplicate elements, the number of appearances of each of them in both lists should match.

assert.ElementsMatch\(t, \[1, 3, 2, 3\], \[1, 3, 3, 2\]\)

<a name="Empty"></a>

## func [Empty](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L58>)

```go
func Empty(t TestingT, object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Empty asserts that the specified object is empty. I.e. nil, "", false, 0 or either a slice or a channel with len == 0.

```
assert.Empty(t, obj)
```

<a name="Equal"></a>

## func [Equal](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L71>)

```go
func Equal(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Equal asserts that two objects are equal.

```
assert.Equal(t, 123, 123)
```

Pointer variable equality is determined based on the equality of the referenced values \(as opposed to the memory addresses\). Function equality cannot be determined and will always fail.

<a name="EqualError"></a>

## func [EqualError](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L82>)

```go
func EqualError(t TestingT, theError error, errString string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

EqualError asserts that a function returned an error \(i.e. not \`nil\`\) and that it is equal to the provided error.

```
actualObj, err := SomeFunction()
assert.EqualError(t, err,  expectedErrorString)
```

<a name="EqualValues"></a>

## func [EqualValues](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L92>)

```go
func EqualValues(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

EqualValues asserts that two objects are equal or convertable to the same types and equal.

```
assert.EqualValues(t, uint32(123), int32(123))
```

<a name="Error"></a>

## func [Error](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L104>)

```go
func Error(t TestingT, err error, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Error asserts that a function returned an error \(i.e. not \`nil\`\).

```
actualObj, err := SomeFunction()
  if assert.Error(t, err) {
    assert.Equal(t, expectedError, err)
  }
```

<a name="Eventually"></a>

## func [Eventually](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L114>)

```go
func Eventually(t TestingT, condition func() bool, waitFor time.Duration, tick time.Duration, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Eventually asserts that given condition will be met in waitFor time, periodically checking target function each tick.

```
assert.Eventually(t, func() bool { return true; }, time.Second, 10*time.Millisecond)
```

<a name="Exactly"></a>

## func [Exactly](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L123>)

```go
func Exactly(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Exactly asserts that two objects are equal in value and type.

```
assert.Exactly(t, int32(123), int64(123))
```

## func [Fail](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L130>)

```go
func Fail(t TestingT, failureMessage string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Fail reports a failure through

```go
func FailNow(t TestingT, failureMessage string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

FailNow fails test

<a name="False"></a>

## func [False](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L146>)

```go
func False(t TestingT, value bool, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

False asserts that the specified value is false.

```
assert.False(t, myBool)
```

<a name="FileExists"></a>

## func [FileExists](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L154>)

```go
func FileExists(t TestingT, path string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

FileExists checks whether a file exists in the given path. It also fails if the path points to a directory or there is an error when trying to check the file.

<a name="Greater"></a>

## func [Greater](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L165>)

```go
func Greater(t TestingT, e1 interface{}, e2 interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Greater asserts that the first element is greater than the second

```
assert.Greater(t, 2, 1)
assert.Greater(t, float64(2), float64(1))
assert.Greater(t, "b", "a")
```

<a name="GreaterOrEqual"></a>

## func [GreaterOrEqual](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L177>)

```go
func GreaterOrEqual(t TestingT, e1 interface{}, e2 interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

GreaterOrEqual asserts that the first element is greater than or equal to the second

```
assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, 2, 1)
assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, 2, 2)
assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, "b", "a")
assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, "b", "b")
```

<a name="HTTPBodyContains"></a>

## func [HTTPBodyContains](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L189>)

```go
func HTTPBodyContains(t TestingT, handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, str interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPBodyContains asserts that a specified handler returns a body that contains a string.

```
assert.HTTPBodyContains(t, myHandler, "GET", "www.google.com", nil, "I'm Feeling Lucky")
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="HTTPBodyNotContains"></a>

## func [HTTPBodyNotContains](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L201>)

```go
func HTTPBodyNotContains(t TestingT, handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, str interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPBodyNotContains asserts that a specified handler returns a body that does not contain a string.

```
assert.HTTPBodyNotContains(t, myHandler, "GET", "www.google.com", nil, "I'm Feeling Lucky")
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="HTTPError"></a>

## func [HTTPError](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L212>)

```go
func HTTPError(t TestingT, handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPError asserts that a specified handler returns an error status code.

```
assert.HTTPError(t, myHandler, "POST", "/a/b/c", url.Values{"a": []string{"b", "c"}}
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="HTTPRedirect"></a>

## func [HTTPRedirect](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L223>)

```go
func HTTPRedirect(t TestingT, handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPRedirect asserts that a specified handler returns a redirect status code.

```
assert.HTTPRedirect(t, myHandler, "GET", "/a/b/c", url.Values{"a": []string{"b", "c"}}
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="HTTPStatusCode"></a>

## func [HTTPStatusCode](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L234>)

```go
func HTTPStatusCode(t TestingT, handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, statuscode int, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPStatusCode asserts that a specified handler returns a specified status code.

```
assert.HTTPStatusCode(t, myHandler, "GET", "/notImplemented", nil, 501)
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="HTTPSuccess"></a>

## func [HTTPSuccess](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L245>)

```go
func HTTPSuccess(t TestingT, handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPSuccess asserts that a specified handler returns a success status code.

```
assert.HTTPSuccess(t, myHandler, "POST", "http://www.google.com", nil)
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="Implements"></a>

## func [Implements](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L254>)

```go
func Implements(t TestingT, interfaceObject interface{}, object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Implements asserts that an object is implemented by the specified interface.

```
assert.Implements(t, (*MyInterface)(nil), new(MyObject))
```

<a name="InDelta"></a>

## func [InDelta](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L263>)

```go
func InDelta(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, delta float64, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

InDelta asserts that the two numerals are within delta of each other.

```
assert.InDelta(t, math.Pi, 22/7.0, 0.01)
```

<a name="InDeltaMapValues"></a>

## func [InDeltaMapValues](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L270>)

```go
func InDeltaMapValues(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, delta float64, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

InDeltaMapValues is the same as InDelta, but it compares all values between two maps. Both maps must have exactly the same keys.

<a name="InDeltaSlice"></a>

## func [InDeltaSlice](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L277>)

```go
func InDeltaSlice(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, delta float64, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

InDeltaSlice is the same as InDelta, except it compares two slices.

<a name="InEpsilon"></a>

## func [InEpsilon](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L284>)

```go
func InEpsilon(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, epsilon float64, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

InEpsilon asserts that expected and actual have a relative error less than epsilon

<a name="InEpsilonSlice"></a>

## func [InEpsilonSlice](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L291>)

```go
func InEpsilonSlice(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, epsilon float64, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

InEpsilonSlice is the same as InEpsilon, except it compares each value from two slices.

<a name="IsType"></a>

## func [IsType](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L298>)

```go
func IsType(t TestingT, expectedType interface{}, object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

IsType asserts that the specified objects are of the same type.

<a name="JSONEq"></a>

## func [JSONEq](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L307>)

```go
func JSONEq(t TestingT, expected string, actual string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

JSONEq asserts that two JSON strings are equivalent.

```
assert.JSONEq(t, `{"hello": "world", "foo": "bar"}`, `{"foo": "bar", "hello": "world"}`)
```

<a name="Len"></a>

## func [Len](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L317>)

```go
func Len(t TestingT, object interface{}, length int, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Len asserts that the specified object has specific length. Len also fails if the object has a type that len\(\) not accept.

```
assert.Len(t, mySlice, 3)
```

<a name="Less"></a>

## func [Less](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L328>)

```go
func Less(t TestingT, e1 interface{}, e2 interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Less asserts that the first element is less than the second

```
assert.Less(t, 1, 2)
assert.Less(t, float64(1), float64(2))
assert.Less(t, "a", "b")
```

<a name="LessOrEqual"></a>

## func [LessOrEqual](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L340>)

```go
func LessOrEqual(t TestingT, e1 interface{}, e2 interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

LessOrEqual asserts that the first element is less than or equal to the second

```
assert.LessOrEqual(t, 1, 2)
assert.LessOrEqual(t, 2, 2)
assert.LessOrEqual(t, "a", "b")
assert.LessOrEqual(t, "b", "b")
```

<a name="Never"></a>

## func [Never](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L350>)

```go
func Never(t TestingT, condition func() bool, waitFor time.Duration, tick time.Duration, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Never asserts that the given condition doesn't satisfy in waitFor time, periodically checking the target function each tick.

```
assert.Never(t, func() bool { return false; }, time.Second, 10*time.Millisecond)
```

<a name="Nil"></a>

## func [Nil](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L359>)

```go
func Nil(t TestingT, object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Nil asserts that the specified object is nil.

```
assert.Nil(t, err)
```

<a name="NoDirExists"></a>

## func [NoDirExists](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L367>)

```go
func NoDirExists(t TestingT, path string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NoDirExists checks whether a directory does not exist in the given path. It fails if the path points to an existing \_directory\_ only.

<a name="NoError"></a>

## func [NoError](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L379>)

```go
func NoError(t TestingT, err error, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NoError asserts that a function returned no error \(i.e. \`nil\`\).

```
actualObj, err := SomeFunction()
  if assert.NoError(t, err) {
    assert.Equal(t, expectedObj, actualObj)
  }
```

<a name="NoFileExists"></a>

## func [NoFileExists](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L387>)

```go
func NoFileExists(t TestingT, path string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NoFileExists checks whether a file does not exist in a given path. It fails if the path points to an existing \_file\_ only.

<a name="NotContains"></a>

## func [NotContains](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L399>)

```go
func NotContains(t TestingT, s interface{}, contains interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotContains asserts that the specified string, list\(array, slice...\) or map does NOT contain the specified substring or element.

```
assert.NotContains(t, "Hello World", "Earth")
assert.NotContains(t, ["Hello", "World"], "Earth")
assert.NotContains(t, {"Hello": "World"}, "Earth")
```

<a name="NotEmpty"></a>

## func [NotEmpty](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L411>)

```go
func NotEmpty(t TestingT, object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotEmpty asserts that the specified object is NOT empty. I.e. not nil, "", false, 0 or either a slice or a channel with len == 0.

```
if assert.NotEmpty(t, obj) {
  assert.Equal(t, "two", obj[1])
}
```

<a name="NotEqual"></a>

## func [NotEqual](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L423>)

```go
func NotEqual(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotEqual asserts that the specified values are NOT equal.

```
assert.NotEqual(t, obj1, obj2)
```

Pointer variable equality is determined based on the equality of the referenced values \(as opposed to the memory addresses\).

<a name="NotEqualValues"></a>

## func [NotEqualValues](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L432>)

```go
func NotEqualValues(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotEqualValues asserts that two objects are not equal even when converted to the same type

```
assert.NotEqualValues(t, obj1, obj2)
```

<a name="NotNil"></a>

## func [NotNil](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L441>)

```go
func NotNil(t TestingT, object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotNil asserts that the specified object is not nil.

```
assert.NotNil(t, err)
```

<a name="NotPanics"></a>

## func [NotPanics](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L450>)

```go
func NotPanics(t TestingT, f assert.PanicTestFunc, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotPanics asserts that the code inside the specified PanicTestFunc does NOT panic.

```
assert.NotPanics(t, func(){ RemainCalm() })
```

<a name="NotRegexp"></a>

## func [NotRegexp](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L460>)

```go
func NotRegexp(t TestingT, rx interface{}, str interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotRegexp asserts that a specified regexp does not match a string.

```
assert.NotRegexp(t, regexp.MustCompile("starts"), "it's starting")
assert.NotRegexp(t, "^start", "it's not starting")
```

<a name="NotSame"></a>

## func [NotSame](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L472>)

```go
func NotSame(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotSame asserts that two pointers do not reference the same object.

```
assert.NotSame(t, ptr1, ptr2)
```

Both arguments must be pointer variables. Pointer variable sameness is determined based on the equality of both type and value.

<a name="NotSubset"></a>

## func [NotSubset](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L482>)

```go
func NotSubset(t TestingT, list interface{}, subset interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotSubset asserts that the specified list\(array, slice...\) contains not all elements given in the specified subset\(array, slice...\).

```
assert.NotSubset(t, [1, 3, 4], [1, 2], "But [1, 3, 4] does not contain [1, 2]")
```

<a name="NotZero"></a>

## func [NotZero](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L489>)

```go
func NotZero(t TestingT, i interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotZero asserts that i is not the zero value for its type.

<a name="Panics"></a>

## func [Panics](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L498>)

```go
func Panics(t TestingT, f assert.PanicTestFunc, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Panics asserts that the code inside the specified PanicTestFunc panics.

```
assert.Panics(t, func(){ GoCrazy() })
```

<a name="PanicsWithError"></a>

## func [PanicsWithError](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L509>)

```go
func PanicsWithError(t TestingT, errString string, f assert.PanicTestFunc, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

PanicsWithError asserts that the code inside the specified PanicTestFunc panics, and that the recovered panic value is an error that satisfies the EqualError comparison.

```
assert.PanicsWithError(t, "crazy error", func(){ GoCrazy() })
```

<a name="PanicsWithValue"></a>

## func [PanicsWithValue](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L519>)

```go
func PanicsWithValue(t TestingT, expected interface{}, f assert.PanicTestFunc, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

PanicsWithValue asserts that the code inside the specified PanicTestFunc panics, and that the recovered panic value equals the expected panic value.

```
assert.PanicsWithValue(t, "crazy error", func(){ GoCrazy() })
```

<a name="Regexp"></a>

## func [Regexp](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L529>)

```go
func Regexp(t TestingT, rx interface{}, str interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Regexp asserts that a specified regexp matches a string.

```
assert.Regexp(t, regexp.MustCompile("start"), "it's starting")
assert.Regexp(t, "start...$", "it's not starting")
```

<a name="Same"></a>

## func [Same](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L541>)

```go
func Same(t TestingT, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Same asserts that two pointers reference the same object.

```
assert.Same(t, ptr1, ptr2)
```

Both arguments must be pointer variables. Pointer variable sameness is determined based on the equality of both type and value.

<a name="Subset"></a>

## func [Subset](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L551>)

```go
func Subset(t TestingT, list interface{}, subset interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Subset asserts that the specified list\(array, slice...\) contains all elements given in the specified subset\(array, slice...\).

```
assert.Subset(t, [1, 2, 3], [1, 2], "But [1, 2, 3] does contain [1, 2]")
```

<a name="True"></a>

## func [True](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L560>)

```go
func True(t TestingT, value bool, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

True asserts that the specified value is true.

```
assert.True(t, myBool)
```

<a name="WithinDuration"></a>

## func [WithinDuration](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L569>)

```go
func WithinDuration(t TestingT, expected time.Time, actual time.Time, delta time.Duration, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

WithinDuration asserts that the two times are within duration delta of each other.

```
assert.WithinDuration(t, time.Now(), time.Now(), 10*time.Second)
```

<a name="YAMLEq"></a>

## func [YAMLEq](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L576>)

```go
func YAMLEq(t TestingT, expected string, actual string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

YAMLEq asserts that two YAML strings are equivalent.

<a name="Zero"></a>

## func [Zero](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require.go#L583>)

```go
func Zero(t TestingT, i interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Zero asserts that i is the zero value for its type.

<a name="Assertions"></a>

## type [Assertions](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/forward_requirements.go#L5-L7>)

Assertions provides assertion methods around the TestingT interface.

```go
type Assertions struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>

### func [New](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/forward_requirements.go#L10>)

```go
func New(t TestingT) *Assertions
```

New makes a new Assertions object for the specified TestingT.

<a name="Assertions.Condition"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Condition](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L17>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Condition(comp assert.Comparison, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Condition uses a Comparison to assert a complex condition.

<a name="Assertions.Contains"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Contains](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L27>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Contains(s interface{}, contains interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Contains asserts that the specified string, list\(array, slice...\) or map contains the specified substring or element.

```
a.Contains("Hello World", "World")
a.Contains(["Hello", "World"], "World")
a.Contains({"Hello": "World"}, "Hello")
```

<a name="Assertions.DirExists"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [DirExists](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L33>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) DirExists(path string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

DirExists checks whether a directory exists in the given path. It also fails if the path is a file rather a directory or there is an error checking whether it exists.

<a name="Assertions.ElementsMatch"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [ElementsMatch](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L42>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) ElementsMatch(listA interface{}, listB interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

ElementsMatch asserts that the specified listA\(array, slice...\) is equal to specified listB\(array, slice...\) ignoring the order of the elements. If there are duplicate elements, the number of appearances of each of them in both lists should match.

a.ElementsMatch\(\[1, 3, 2, 3\], \[1, 3, 3, 2\]\)

<a name="Assertions.Empty"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Empty](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L50>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Empty(object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Empty asserts that the specified object is empty. I.e. nil, "", false, 0 or either a slice or a channel with len == 0.

```
a.Empty(obj)
```

<a name="Assertions.Equal"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Equal](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L61>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Equal(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Equal asserts that two objects are equal.

```
a.Equal(123, 123)
```

Pointer variable equality is determined based on the equality of the referenced values \(as opposed to the memory addresses\). Function equality cannot be determined and will always fail.

<a name="Assertions.EqualError"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [EqualError](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L70>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) EqualError(theError error, errString string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

EqualError asserts that a function returned an error \(i.e. not \`nil\`\) and that it is equal to the provided error.

```
actualObj, err := SomeFunction()
a.EqualError(err,  expectedErrorString)
```

<a name="Assertions.EqualValues"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [EqualValues](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L78>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) EqualValues(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

EqualValues asserts that two objects are equal or convertable to the same types and equal.

```
a.EqualValues(uint32(123), int32(123))
```

<a name="Assertions.Error"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Error](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L88>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Error(err error, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Error asserts that a function returned an error \(i.e. not \`nil\`\).

```
actualObj, err := SomeFunction()
  if a.Error(err) {
    assert.Equal(t, expectedError, err)
  }
```

<a name="Assertions.Eventually"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Eventually](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L96>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Eventually(condition func() bool, waitFor time.Duration, tick time.Duration, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Eventually asserts that given condition will be met in waitFor time, periodically checking target function each tick.

```
a.Eventually(func() bool { return true; }, time.Second, 10*time.Millisecond)
```

<a name="Assertions.Exactly"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Exactly](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L103>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Exactly(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Exactly asserts that two objects are equal in value and type.

```
a.Exactly(int32(123), int64(123))
```

<a name="Assertions.Fail"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Fail](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L108>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Fail(failureMessage string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Fail reports a failure through

<a name="Assertions.FailNow"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [FailNow](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L113>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) FailNow(failureMessage string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

FailNow fails test

<a name="Assertions.False"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [False](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L120>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) False(value bool, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

False asserts that the specified value is false.

```
a.False(myBool)
```

<a name="Assertions.FileExists"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [FileExists](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L126>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) FileExists(path string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

FileExists checks whether a file exists in the given path. It also fails if the path points to a directory or there is an error when trying to check the file.

<a name="Assertions.Greater"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Greater](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L135>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Greater(e1 interface{}, e2 interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Greater asserts that the first element is greater than the second

```
a.Greater(2, 1)
a.Greater(float64(2), float64(1))
a.Greater("b", "a")
```

<a name="Assertions.GreaterOrEqual"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [GreaterOrEqual](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L145>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) GreaterOrEqual(e1 interface{}, e2 interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

GreaterOrEqual asserts that the first element is greater than or equal to the second

```
a.GreaterOrEqual(2, 1)
a.GreaterOrEqual(2, 2)
a.GreaterOrEqual("b", "a")
a.GreaterOrEqual("b", "b")
```

<a name="Assertions.HTTPBodyContains"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [HTTPBodyContains](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L155>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) HTTPBodyContains(handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, str interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPBodyContains asserts that a specified handler returns a body that contains a string.

```
a.HTTPBodyContains(myHandler, "GET", "www.google.com", nil, "I'm Feeling Lucky")
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="Assertions.HTTPBodyNotContains"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [HTTPBodyNotContains](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L165>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) HTTPBodyNotContains(handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, str interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPBodyNotContains asserts that a specified handler returns a body that does not contain a string.

```
a.HTTPBodyNotContains(myHandler, "GET", "www.google.com", nil, "I'm Feeling Lucky")
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="Assertions.HTTPError"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [HTTPError](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L174>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) HTTPError(handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPError asserts that a specified handler returns an error status code.

```
a.HTTPError(myHandler, "POST", "/a/b/c", url.Values{"a": []string{"b", "c"}}
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="Assertions.HTTPRedirect"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [HTTPRedirect](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L183>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) HTTPRedirect(handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPRedirect asserts that a specified handler returns a redirect status code.

```
a.HTTPRedirect(myHandler, "GET", "/a/b/c", url.Values{"a": []string{"b", "c"}}
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="Assertions.HTTPStatusCode"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [HTTPStatusCode](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L192>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) HTTPStatusCode(handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, statuscode int, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPStatusCode asserts that a specified handler returns a specified status code.

```
a.HTTPStatusCode(myHandler, "GET", "/notImplemented", nil, 501)
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="Assertions.HTTPSuccess"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [HTTPSuccess](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L201>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) HTTPSuccess(handler http.HandlerFunc, method string, url string, values url.Values, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

HTTPSuccess asserts that a specified handler returns a success status code.

```
a.HTTPSuccess(myHandler, "POST", "http://www.google.com", nil)
```

Returns whether the assertion was successful \(true\) or not \(false\).

<a name="Assertions.Implements"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Implements](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L208>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Implements(interfaceObject interface{}, object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Implements asserts that an object is implemented by the specified interface.

```
a.Implements((*MyInterface)(nil), new(MyObject))
```



```go
func (a *Assertions) InDeltaSlice(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, delta float64, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

InDeltaSlice is the same as InDelta, except it compares two slices.


<a name="Assertions.InEpsilonSlice"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [InEpsilonSlice](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L235>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) InEpsilonSlice(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, epsilon float64, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

InEpsilonSlice is the same as InEpsilon, except it compares each value from two slices.

<a name="Assertions.IsType"></a>

```go
func (a *Assertions) IsType(expectedType interface{}, object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

IsType asserts that the specified objects are of the same type.

<a name="Assertions.JSONEq"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [JSONEq](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L247>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) JSONEq(expected string, actual string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

JSONEq asserts that two JSON strings are equivalent.

```
a.JSONEq(`{"hello": "world", "foo": "bar"}`, `{"foo": "bar", "hello": "world"}`)
```

<a name="Assertions.Len"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Len](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L255>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Len(object interface{}, length int, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Len asserts that the specified object has specific length. Len also fails if the object has a type that len\(\) not accept.

```
a.Len(mySlice, 3)
```

<a name="Assertions.Less"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Less](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L264>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Less(e1 interface{}, e2 interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Less asserts that the first element is less than the second

```
a.Less(1, 2)
a.Less(float64(1), float64(2))
a.Less("a", "b")
```

<a name="Assertions.LessOrEqual"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [LessOrEqual](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L274>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) LessOrEqual(e1 interface{}, e2 interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

LessOrEqual asserts that the first element is less than or equal to the second

```
a.LessOrEqual(1, 2)
a.LessOrEqual(2, 2)
a.LessOrEqual("a", "b")
a.LessOrEqual("b", "b")
```

<a name="Assertions.Never"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Never](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L282>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Never(condition func() bool, waitFor time.Duration, tick time.Duration, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Never asserts that the given condition doesn't satisfy in waitFor time, periodically checking the target function each tick.

```
a.Never(func() bool { return false; }, time.Second, 10*time.Millisecond)
```

<a name="Assertions.Nil"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Nil](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L289>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Nil(object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Nil asserts that the specified object is nil.

```
a.Nil(err)
```

<a name="Assertions.NoDirExists"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NoDirExists](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L295>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NoDirExists(path string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NoDirExists checks whether a directory does not exist in the given path. It fails if the path points to an existing \_directory\_ only.

<a name="Assertions.NoError"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NoError](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L305>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NoError(err error, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NoError asserts that a function returned no error \(i.e. \`nil\`\).

```
actualObj, err := SomeFunction()
  if a.NoError(err) {
    assert.Equal(t, expectedObj, actualObj)
  }
```

<a name="Assertions.NoFileExists"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NoFileExists](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L311>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NoFileExists(path string, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NoFileExists checks whether a file does not exist in a given path. It fails if the path points to an existing \_file\_ only.

<a name="Assertions.NotContains"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotContains](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L321>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotContains(s interface{}, contains interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotContains asserts that the specified string, list\(array, slice...\) or map does NOT contain the specified substring or element.

```
a.NotContains("Hello World", "Earth")
a.NotContains(["Hello", "World"], "Earth")
a.NotContains({"Hello": "World"}, "Earth")
```

<a name="Assertions.NotEmpty"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotEmpty](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L331>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotEmpty(object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotEmpty asserts that the specified object is NOT empty. I.e. not nil, "", false, 0 or either a slice or a channel with len == 0.

```
if a.NotEmpty(obj) {
  assert.Equal(t, "two", obj[1])
}
```

<a name="Assertions.NotEqual"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotEqual](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L341>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotEqual(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotEqual asserts that the specified values are NOT equal.

```
a.NotEqual(obj1, obj2)
```

Pointer variable equality is determined based on the equality of the referenced values \(as opposed to the memory addresses\).

<a name="Assertions.NotEqualValues"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotEqualValues](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L348>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotEqualValues(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotEqualValues asserts that two objects are not equal even when converted to the same type

```
a.NotEqualValues(obj1, obj2)
```

<a name="Assertions.NotNil"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotNil](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L355>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotNil(object interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotNil asserts that the specified object is not nil.

```
a.NotNil(err)
```

<a name="Assertions.NotPanics"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotPanics](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L362>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotPanics(f assert.PanicTestFunc, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotPanics asserts that the code inside the specified PanicTestFunc does NOT panic.

```
a.NotPanics(func(){ RemainCalm() })
```

<a name="Assertions.NotRegexp"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotRegexp](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L370>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotRegexp(rx interface{}, str interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotRegexp asserts that a specified regexp does not match a string.

```
a.NotRegexp(regexp.MustCompile("starts"), "it's starting")
a.NotRegexp("^start", "it's not starting")
```

<a name="Assertions.NotSame"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotSame](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L380>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotSame(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotSame asserts that two pointers do not reference the same object.

```
a.NotSame(ptr1, ptr2)
```

Both arguments must be pointer variables. Pointer variable sameness is determined based on the equality of both type and value.

<a name="Assertions.NotSubset"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotSubset](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L388>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotSubset(list interface{}, subset interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotSubset asserts that the specified list\(array, slice...\) contains not all elements given in the specified subset\(array, slice...\).

```
a.NotSubset([1, 3, 4], [1, 2], "But [1, 3, 4] does not contain [1, 2]")
```

<a name="Assertions.NotZero"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [NotZero](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L393>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) NotZero(i interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

NotZero asserts that i is not the zero value for its type.

<a name="Assertions.Panics"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Panics](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L400>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Panics(f assert.PanicTestFunc, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Panics asserts that the code inside the specified PanicTestFunc panics.

```
a.Panics(func(){ GoCrazy() })
```

<a name="Assertions.PanicsWithError"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [PanicsWithError](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L409>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) PanicsWithError(errString string, f assert.PanicTestFunc, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

PanicsWithError asserts that the code inside the specified PanicTestFunc panics, and that the recovered panic value is an error that satisfies the EqualError comparison.

```
a.PanicsWithError("crazy error", func(){ GoCrazy() })
```

<a name="Assertions.PanicsWithValue"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [PanicsWithValue](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L417>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) PanicsWithValue(expected interface{}, f assert.PanicTestFunc, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

PanicsWithValue asserts that the code inside the specified PanicTestFunc panics, and that the recovered panic value equals the expected panic value.

```
a.PanicsWithValue("crazy error", func(){ GoCrazy() })
```

<a name="Assertions.Regexp"></a>

```go
func (a *Assertions) Regexp(rx interface{}, str interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Regexp asserts that a specified regexp matches a string.

```
a.Regexp(regexp.MustCompile("start"), "it's starting")
a.Regexp("start...$", "it's not starting")
```

```go
func (a *Assertions) Same(expected interface{}, actual interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Same asserts that two pointers reference the same object.

```
a.Same(ptr1, ptr2)
```

Both arguments must be pointer variables. Pointer variable sameness is determined based on the equality of both type and value.

<a name="Assertions.Subset"></a>

### func \(\*Assertions\) [Subset](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/require_forward.go#L443>)

```go
func (a *Assertions) Subset(list interface{}, subset interface{}, msgAndArgs ...interface{})
```

Subset asserts that the specified list\(array, slice...\) contains all elements given in the specified subset\(array, slice...\).

```
a.Subset([1, 2, 3], [1, 2], "But [1, 2, 3] does contain [1, 2]")
```


True asserts that the specified value is true.

<a name="TestingT"></a>

## type [TestingT](<https://github.com/tj/assert/blob/master/requirements.go#L4-L7>)

TestingT is an interface wrapper around \*testing.T

```go
type TestingT interface {
    Errorf(format string, args ...interface{})
    FailNow()
}
```
